page: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, featuring 8 physical cores and 16 dedicated vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all rank in the top 10% tier. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also demonstrate average performance.

For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds and strong prompt processing speeds when running small 135M parameter language models. However, performance drops to average for medium 7B models, and prompt processing for large 70B models is categorized in the bottom 10%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput consistently register in the average tier.

Given its high memory density of 16 GB per vCPU and dedicated CPU allocation, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the contrast between top-tier single-core execution and average multi-core scaling. It is suitable for memory-intensive applications, database caching layers, small model LLM text generation, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring large memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Easv7 family, featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per core, and lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in single-core CPU execution, single-threaded gzip compression, and small-model LLM text generation. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier, while large-model LLM prompt processing is poor. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that prioritize high memory-to-vCPU ratios over local storage. It is suited for database caching, memory-bound enterprise applications, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for memory-intensive workloads and database caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage, but compatible with Premium Storage options
Suited for database caching, memory-intensive applications, and small LLM inference
